item.title: Parents: Tell us how are you balancing work and childcare this summer
item.summary: With the cost of holiday childcare rising and many parents juggling work with looking after children, we’d like to hear how you’re managing this summer The summer holidays have begun, bringing the annual challenge many parents face in balancing work and looking after school-aged children. New research suggests arranging six weeks of holiday childcare can cost working parents up to £1,145 per child , while almost three-quarters of parents say the summer childcare juggle damages their career . Are you paying for holiday clubs or childminders, relying on family and friends or working from home while caring for children? Are you taking annual leave, reducing your hours or working early mornings and late evenings to fit everything in? Continue reading...
